# HAMR Framework — Complete Package Reference

> Full API documentation for hamr/pkg packages.
> Import base: github.com/FyrmForge/hamr/pkg/<name>

## Glossary

- **templ**: Go HTML templating language (NOT Go's html/template). Compiled to Go.
  Files use .templ extension. Components are Go functions returning templ.Component.
- **HTMX**: JavaScript library for HTML-over-the-wire. Attributes like hx-post,
  hx-swap, hx-target drive server interaction without writing JS.
- **OOB swap**: HTMX out-of-band swap. Element with hx-swap-oob="true" replaces
  a matching element by ID anywhere in the DOM, independent of the main swap target.
- **PRG pattern**: Post-Redirect-Get. POST handlers redirect with 303 to prevent
  form resubmission.
- **PHC format**: Password Hash Competition format string for Argon2id hashes.
- **Echo context (echo.Context)**: The request/response wrapper in Echo v4.
  NOT Go's context.Context. Use c.Request().Context() for the stdlib context.
- **functional options**: Constructor pattern using variadic Option functions
  (e.g., server.New(server.WithPort(8080))).
- **Store interface**: Repository pattern used in scaffolded projects.
  Generated projects define a Store interface in their internal/repo/repo.go;
  it is not part of the hamr framework itself.
- **hamr dev**: Dev server with file watching, auto-rebuild, and live reload.
  Mirrors its recent output to a rolling log file (default
  `.hamr/dev_logs.txt`, 200 lines) for LLM consumption. The file contains
  `[hamr dev]` messages plus watched command/daemon stdout-stderr with terminal
  escapes stripped. The injected reload script also pipes browser
  `window.console.*` calls + uncaught JS errors + unhandled promise
  rejections + resource-load failures (`<img>`/`<script>`/`<link>` 404s)
  + CSP violations back to the dev server over a WebSocket at
  `/__hamr/console`; the dev server logs them tagged `[site:console]`
  in the same TUI tab and `dev_logs.txt` as backend output, in arrival
  order. Wire format is JSON `{level, msg, src?}` per frame, batched
  client-side every ~100ms (`level` ∈ `log`/`info`/`debug`/`warn`/
  `error`/`rejection`/`resource`/`csp`; `src` populated only for
  uncaught errors with file:line:col). Browser-engine warnings printed
  directly to DevTools (deprecation notices, mixed-content, autoplay
  blocked, the formatted "Failed to load resource" lines) and
  `fetch`/`XHR` network errors are not catchable from page JS, so they
  aren't captured. Gated by `[dev].hamr_console_capture` (default
  true): the SSE config payload carries a `console_capture` flag and
  the JS patches console + opens the WS only when true; setting
  `[dev].hamr_console_capture = false` also prevents the server from
  mounting the `/__hamr/console` endpoint. Set
  `[dev].hamr_console_filter = true` to drop frames whose message
  contains `[hamr]` (the reload script's own chatter — `[hamr] page
  swapped`, `[hamr] CSS reloaded`, etc.); default false shows
  everything. Configure via `log_file` and `log_file_max_lines` in
  `[dev]`; set `log_file = "none"` to disable. The TUI adds an `M` hotkey to
  toggle the MCP gateway (see **hamr mcp**); status bar shows `MCP on/<n>` or
  `MCP off`.
- **hamr mcp**: MCP (Model Context Protocol) bridge that lets an AI agent
  (Claude Code, Codex, opencode) drive a running `hamr dev`. Two hops:
  agent ⟷ `hamr mcp` over stdio, then `hamr mcp` ⟷ `hamr dev` over localhost
  HTTP at `/__hamr/mcp/{tool}`. Enabled via `[dev.mcp]` (default off) + the `M`
  runtime kill-switch in the TUI. When enabled the gateway writes a per-run
  bearer token + resolved proxy URL to `.hamr/dev.json` (0600, gitignored); the
  bridge reads it (resolving the project via `--project` or the nearest
  `hamr.toml` from cwd) and authenticates each call. Auth is token-only and the
  `/__hamr/mcp/*` namespace is separate from the browser `/__hamr/*` routes, so
  the kill-switch and permissions never affect the dev panel. Permissions are
  per-area in `[dev.mcp.access]` (`read`/`write`/omit=deny; `write` implies
  `read`): `dev` (dev.info), `logs` (logs.read [ANSI-stripped, timestamped,
  rule prefix-matched so rule="site" catches site:build/site:run], console.read
  [structured timestamped frames: time/level/msg/src], http.read [dev-proxy
  request log: method/path/status/durationMs incl /__hamr/*, static, SSE/WS —
  the proxy-level view the app's own access log can't give]), `docker`
  (logs/status read; restart/wipe write), `mail` (list/get read; clear/ingest
  write), `build` (rule.run, rebuild.all, make.run — write-only), `stripe`
  (list read; complete/expire/refund write). The gateway advertises only
  granted tools and re-checks permission per call (the security boundary; the
  bridge's filtering is UX). Async semantics: reads return immediately;
  docker.restart/wipe and rule.run/rebuild.all dispatch (poll docker.status /
  logs.read) — or pass `wait:true` (+ `wait_timeout`, default 60s) to
  docker.restart/wipe to block until running/healthy and get final statuses;
  make.run returns inline `output` for fast targets, else waits `make_wait`
  (default 20s) and returns `status:running` for slow ones, and is constrained
  by `make_targets` (empty=all allowed). Every call is audit-logged to `.hamr/mcp_logs.txt`
  (`log_file`; `"none"` disables); a dedicated `mcp` TUI tab (last in the `Tab`
  cycle) shows one line per request (reads included), and mutations are also
  tagged `[mcp]` in the main log tab. Setup: `hamr mcp install --client claude|codex|opencode` (auto-detects
  installed agents when omitted; `--dry-run` previews) writes the per-client
  config, merging existing entries and idempotent — Claude `.mcp.json` and
  opencode `opencode.json` are project-scoped; Codex `~/.codex/config.toml` is
  global and gets `--project` pinned to this project. Requires `hamr` on PATH
  and `[proxy]` configured (the gateway lives on the proxy mux).
- **hamr ai capture**: Browser screenshot capture with HTML/text/metadata sidecars.
- **hamr ai upgrade**: Git-based scaffold upgrade report. Clones the HAMR repo and diffs between version tags to produce a unified diff and stat summary of all changes between the project's baseline and current HAMR.
- **.hamr/ai/**: Playground directory for all AI command artifacts (captures, upgrade reports). Gitignored. Configurable via `[ai] dir` in hamr.toml.
- **hamr locale gen**: Code generator for type-safe i18n accessors.
- **hamr gen static**: Content-based static asset fingerprinting. Reads from
  static/, writes fingerprinted copies to dist/, and generates a Go source file
  with the manifest baked in at compile time. Config in [static] section of
  hamr.toml. `--clean` removes dist/ and resets the manifest.
- **hamr completion install**: Installs shell completion scripts for bash, zsh, or fish. Auto-detects shell from $SHELL. Supports --system for system-wide install (requires root) and --shell to override detection.
- **respond.Redirect**: HTMX-aware redirect that sets HX-Redirect for HTMX
  requests and falls back to HTTP 303.

---

## Directives

How hamr is meant to be used. Lean rules with rationale, do/don't examples, and tracked gaps where the codebase doesn't yet match.

- `docs/guide/directives/` — directory of directives, one per file
- `docs/guide/directives/gaps.md` — known drifts between code and directives

---

## Core Packages

### config

> Package config provides typed accessors for environment variables with
> sensible defaults and panic-on-missing semantics.
> For .env file loading, use the godotenv/autoload blank import in your main
> package: `import _ "github.com/joho/godotenv/autoload"`

```go
func ParseBaseURL(raw string) (origin, hostname string, err error)
```
Validates a raw base URL string and returns the origin (scheme://host, including
port if present) and the hostname (without port). An empty input is valid and
returns zero values (optional in dev).

```go
func GetEnvOrDefault(key, def string) string
```
Returns the value of the environment variable named by key, or `def` if the
variable is unset or empty.

```go
func GetEnvOrPanic(key string) string
```
Returns the value of the environment variable named by key. Panics if the
variable is unset or empty.

```go
func GetEnvOrDefaultInt(key string, def int) int
```
Returns the environment variable as an int, falling back to `def` if unset,
empty, or not a valid integer.

```go
func GetEnvOrDefaultBool(key string, def bool) bool
```
Returns the environment variable as a bool, falling back to `def` if unset,
empty, or not a valid boolean.

```go
func GetEnvOrDefaultDuration(key string, def time.Duration) time.Duration
```
Returns the environment variable as a time.Duration, falling back to `def` if
unset, empty, or not a valid duration string.

```go
func GetEnvCSV(key string) []string
```
Returns the environment variable parsed as a comma-separated list (whitespace
trimmed, empty entries dropped). Returns `nil` when unset/empty.

---

### server

> Package server wraps Echo v4 with functional options, production-safe
> defaults, and graceful shutdown.

#### Types

```go
type Server struct{ /* unexported fields */ }
type Option func(*Server)
```

#### Constructor

```go
func New(opts ...Option) (*Server, error)
```
Creates a Server with sensible defaults, applies options, and configures
production middleware. Returns an error if any option value is invalid.

#### Option Functions

```go
func WithHost(host string) Option                        // bind address
func WithPort(port int) Option                           // listen port (1-65535)
func WithDevMode(dev bool) Option                        // skip security headers in dev
func WithMiddleware(mw ...echo.MiddlewareFunc) Option    // append global middleware
func WithStaticDir(path string) Option                   // serve static/ at /static
func WithStaticDistDir(path string) Option               // dist/ takes priority over static/
func WithEmbeddedStatic(fsys fs.FS, prefix string) Option // serve from embed.FS
func WithErrorHandler(h echo.HTTPErrorHandler) Option    // custom error handler
func WithTimeout(d time.Duration) Option                 // request context timeout
func WithMaxBodySize(size string) Option                 // e.g. "2M", "500K"
func WithShutdownTimeout(d time.Duration) Option         // graceful shutdown (default 10s)
func WithGeneratedDir(dir string) Option                 // pre-rendered static pages dir
func WithTrustedProxies(cidrs ...string) Option          // proxy CIDRs trusted for X-Forwarded-For; unset = ignore XFF (RealIP unspoofable)
```

Behind a proxy you MUST pass its range (`WithTrustedProxies` / `TRUSTED_PROXIES`
env, comma-separated CIDRs) or `RealIP()` returns the proxy IP, not the client:
nginx/Caddy on-host → `127.0.0.1/32` (loopback is NOT auto-trusted); Docker →
the proxy's subnet; AWS ALB/GCP LB → the VPC/subnet CIDR; PaaS (Railway/Fly/
Render) → the platform's internal proxy range, which you often can't pin and may
change, so verify it. Requirements: the proxy must actually emit
`X-Forwarded-For`; never trust `0.0.0.0/0` (re-enables IP spoofing). Verify by
dumping `c.Request().RemoteAddr` (the peer → CIDR to trust) vs `c.RealIP()`.

#### Methods

```go
func (s *Server) Echo() *echo.Echo                       // underlying Echo instance
func (s *Server) Addr() string                           // "host:port"
func (s *Server) Start() error                           // blocks, graceful shutdown on signal
func (s *Server) Shutdown(ctx context.Context) error     // manual shutdown

// Route registration
func (s *Server) GET(path string, h echo.HandlerFunc, m ...echo.MiddlewareFunc) *echo.Route
func (s *Server) POST(path string, h echo.HandlerFunc, m ...echo.MiddlewareFunc) *echo.Route
func (s *Server) PUT(path string, h echo.HandlerFunc, m ...echo.MiddlewareFunc) *echo.Route
func (s *Server) DELETE(path string, h echo.HandlerFunc, m ...echo.MiddlewareFunc) *echo.Route
func (s *Server) PATCH(path string, h echo.HandlerFunc, m ...echo.MiddlewareFunc) *echo.Route
func (s *Server) Group(prefix string, m ...echo.MiddlewareFunc) *echo.Group

// Static page generation
func (s *Server) StaticPage(path string, handler echo.HandlerFunc)
func (s *Server) GenerateStatic(dir string) error
```

---

### logging

> Package logging provides context-aware structured logging wrapping log/slog.
> Production mode uses JSON handler; dev mode uses coloured tint handler.

```go
func New(production bool) *slog.Logger
```
Creates a new logger. JSON handler for production, tint handler for dev.

```go
func FromContext(ctx context.Context) *slog.Logger
```
Extracts the logger from ctx. Falls back to the package-level default.

```go
func WithLogger(ctx context.Context, l *slog.Logger) context.Context
```
Returns a copy of ctx carrying l.

```go
func With(ctx context.Context, args ...any) context.Context
```
Returns a copy of ctx whose logger has the supplied slog attributes appended.

---

### ctx

> Package ctx provides type-safe context key helpers for Echo handlers.
> Wraps Echo's context Set/Get with generics to avoid type assertion boilerplate.

#### Types

```go
type Key[T any] struct{ /* unexported: name string */ }
```

#### Functions

```go
func NewKey[T any](name string) Key[T]
func (k Key[T]) String() string

func Set[T any](c echo.Context, key Key[T], value T)
func Get[T any](c echo.Context, key Key[T]) (T, bool)
func MustGet[T any](c echo.Context, key Key[T]) T

func GetAs[T any](c echo.Context, key Key[any]) (T, bool)
func MustGetAs[T any](c echo.Context, key Key[any]) T
```

#### Pre-defined Keys

```go
var (
    SubjectIDKey  = NewKey[string]("subject_id")
    SubjectKey    = NewKey[any]("subject")
    SessionKey    = NewKey[any]("session")
    RequestIDKey  = NewKey[string]("request_id")
    FlashKey      = NewKey[any]("flash")
    TranslatorKey = NewKey[any]("i18n_translator")
    LocaleKey     = NewKey[string]("i18n_locale")
)
```

---

### respond

> Package respond provides HTTP response helpers for HTMX-first applications.
> Renders templ components or JSON payloads via Echo.

#### Functions

```go
func HTML(c echo.Context, status int, component templ.Component) error
```
Renders a templ component with the given status code.

```go
func JSON(c echo.Context, status int, data any) error
```
Sends a JSON response with the given status code.

```go
func Redirect(c echo.Context, url string) error
```
HTMX-aware redirect. For HTMX requests: sets HX-Redirect header, returns 200.
For regular requests: returns 303.

#### Pagination

```go
func ParsePagination(c echo.Context, defaultSize int) (page, size int)
```
Reads page and size query params. Defaults page to 1, clamps size to [1, 100].

```go
func NewPage(page, size, total int) Page
```
Computes pagination metadata.

```go
type Page struct {
    Number     int  `json:"number"`
    Size       int  `json:"size"`
    Total      int  `json:"total"`
    TotalPages int  `json:"total_pages"`
    HasNext    bool `json:"has_next"`
    HasPrev    bool `json:"has_prev"`
}

type PagedResponse[T any] struct {
    Data []T  `json:"data"`
    Page Page `json:"page"`
}
```

---

### validate

> Package validate provides pure-function validators that return "" on success
> or a human-readable error message string on failure. The Form API lets you
> define field rules once and reuse them for full-form and HTMX per-field validation.

#### Constants

```go
const (
    MsgRequired          = "This field is required"
    MsgEmailInvalid      = "Invalid email address"
    MsgPhoneInvalid      = "Invalid phone number"
    MsgURLInvalid        = "Invalid URL"
    MsgMinLength         = "Too short"
    MsgMaxLength         = "Too long"
    MsgOneOf             = "Invalid selection"
    MsgIntRange          = "Value out of range"
    MsgMinAge            = "Does not meet minimum age requirement"
    MsgMaxAge            = "Exceeds maximum age"
    MsgPasswordWeak      = "Password is too weak"
    MsgHasUpper          = "Must contain an uppercase letter"
    MsgHasLower          = "Must contain a lowercase letter"
    MsgHasDigit          = "Must contain a digit"
    MsgHasSpecial        = "Must contain a special character"
    MsgValidatorNotFound = "Unknown validator"
)
```

#### Type Aliases and Types

```go
type Rule = func(string) string                          // alias — existing validators satisfy it
type CtxRule = func(echo.Context, string) string          // context-aware rule
type FieldRenderer func(c echo.Context, field, errMsg string) error

type PasswordRequirement struct {
    Description string
    Met         bool
}
```

#### Core Validators

Each validator returns "" for valid, error string for invalid.
Every validator has a `*Msg` variant that accepts a custom error message.

```go
func Required(value string) string
func RequiredMsg(value, msg string) string

func EmptyOr(fn func(string) string) func(string) string  // wraps validator for optional fields

func Email(value string) string
func EmailMsg(value, msg string) string

func Phone(value string) string                // accepts optional '+' + 7-15 digits
func PhoneMsg(value, msg string) string

func URL(value string) string         // absolute http/https only (rejects javascript:/data:/vbscript: XSS schemes)
func URLMsg(value, msg string) string

func MinLength(value string, min int) string
func MinLengthMsg(value string, min int, msg string) string

func MaxLength(value string, max int) string
func MaxLengthMsg(value string, max int, msg string) string

func OneOf(value string, options ...string) string
func OneOfMsg(value, msg string, options ...string) string

func IntRange(value int, min, max int) string
func IntRangeMsg(value, min, max int, msg string) string
```

#### Curried Constructors

Return `func(string) string` for use as Form rules:

```go
func MinLen(n int) Rule
func MaxLen(n int) Rule
func In(options ...string) Rule
func AgeMin(minAge int) Rule
func AgeMax(maxAge int) Rule
```

#### Rule Helpers

```go
func WithMsg(rule Rule, msg string) Rule       // wraps rule to override error message
func RunRules(value string, rules ...Rule) string  // runs rules in order, returns first error
```

#### Date/Age Validators

```go
func MinAge(birthDate string, minAge int) string      // birthDate: YYYY-MM-DD
func MinAgeMsg(birthDate string, minAge int, msg string) string
func MaxAge(birthDate string, maxAge int) string
func MaxAgeMsg(birthDate string, maxAge int, msg string) string
```

#### Character-Class Validators

Individual checks for composable password rules:

```go
func HasUpper(value string) string       // "Must contain an uppercase letter"
func HasUpperMsg(value, msg string) string

func HasLower(value string) string       // "Must contain a lowercase letter"
func HasLowerMsg(value, msg string) string

func HasDigit(value string) string       // "Must contain a digit"
func HasDigitMsg(value, msg string) string

func HasSpecial(value string) string     // "Must contain a special character"
func HasSpecialMsg(value, msg string) string
```

#### Password Strength

```go
func CheckPasswordRequirements(password string) []PasswordRequirement
func PasswordStrength(password string) string
func PasswordStrengthMsg(password, msg string) string
```

#### URL Normalisation

```go
func NormalizeURL(value string) string  // prepends "https://" when no scheme
```

#### Custom Validator Registry

```go
func Register(name string, fn func(string) string)
func Run(name, value string) string  // returns MsgValidatorNotFound if not registered
```

#### Form API

Define field rules once, use for both full-form and HTMX per-field validation:

```go
type FormOption interface{ apply(*formConfig) }

func NewForm(opts ...FormOption) Form
func WithOOBRenderer(fn FieldRenderer) FormOption
func WithGeneralError(msg string) FormOption
func WithTrim(on bool) FormOption
func WithShortCircuit(on bool) FormOption
```

Field definitions return FieldBuilder (satisfies FormOption):

```go
func Field(name string, rules ...Rule) FieldBuilder
func FieldMsg(name, msg string, rules ...Rule) FieldBuilder
func (fb FieldBuilder) WithRenderer(fn FieldRenderer) FieldBuilder
func (fb FieldBuilder) WithCtx(rules ...CtxRule) FieldBuilder
```

Form methods:

```go
func (f Form) Validate(c echo.Context) map[string]string          // nil when all pass
func (f Form) ValidationHandler(paramName string) echo.HandlerFunc // HTMX per-field endpoint
```

Usage pattern:

```go
formRules := validate.NewForm(
    validate.WithOOBRenderer(form.OOBValidator),
    validate.WithTrim(true),
    validate.Field("name", validate.Required, validate.MinLen(2)),
    validate.Field("email", validate.Required, validate.Email),
    validate.FieldMsg("password", "Password too weak", validate.Required, validate.PasswordStrength),
)

// Full form validation
errs := formRules.Validate(c)  // map[string]string or nil

// HTMX per-field (one route for all fields)
group.POST("/validate/:field", formRules.ValidationHandler("field"))
```

Short-circuit: per-field always on (first rule failure stops). Per-form configurable
via WithShortCircuit (default off — validates all fields).

Cross-field via WithCtx (runs after standard rules, only if all pass):

```go
validate.Field("password_confirm", validate.Required).
    WithCtx(func(c echo.Context, value string) string {
        if value != c.FormValue("password") {
            return "Passwords do not match"
        }
        return ""
    })
```

WithTrim only trims the field being validated — values read via `c.FormValue` inside
a CtxRule are untrimmed; apply `strings.TrimSpace` manually if needed.

When the field is exposed via `ValidationHandler`, the per-field HTMX request only
sends the triggering input's value by default. Other fields referenced inside the
CtxRule must be pulled in with `hx-include`:

```html
<input name="password_confirm" hx-post="/register/validate/password_confirm"
       hx-include="[name='password']" hx-swap="none" />
```

Without `hx-include`, `c.FormValue("password")` returns "". The validation is also
asymmetric — editing the password field doesn't re-validate the confirm field unless
its input is wired with a matching cross-trigger, or you rely on full-form
`Validate(c)` at submit time.

---

### htmx

> Package htmx provides request detection and response header helpers for htmx.
> All functions operate on standard net/http types.

#### Request Headers (constants)

```go
const (
    HeaderRequest        = "HX-Request"
    HeaderBoosted        = "HX-Boosted"
    HeaderTrigger        = "HX-Trigger"
    HeaderTarget         = "HX-Target"
    HeaderCurrentURL     = "HX-Current-URL"
    HeaderPrompt         = "HX-Prompt"
    HeaderHistoryRestore = "HX-History-Restore-Request"
)
```

#### Response Headers (constants)

```go
const (
    HeaderRedirect           = "HX-Redirect"
    HeaderTriggerResponse    = "HX-Trigger"
    HeaderTriggerAfterSettle = "HX-Trigger-After-Settle"
    HeaderTriggerAfterSwap   = "HX-Trigger-After-Swap"
    HeaderReswap             = "HX-Reswap"
    HeaderRetarget           = "HX-Retarget"
    HeaderRefresh            = "HX-Refresh"
    HeaderPushURL            = "HX-Push-Url"
    HeaderReplaceURL         = "HX-Replace-Url"
)
```

#### Request Detection

```go
func IsHTMX(r *http.Request) bool
func IsBoosted(r *http.Request) bool
func GetTrigger(r *http.Request) string
func GetTarget(r *http.Request) string
```

#### Response Helpers

```go
func Redirect(w http.ResponseWriter, url string)
func Trigger(w http.ResponseWriter, events ...string)
func TriggerAfterSettle(w http.ResponseWriter, events ...string)
func TriggerAfterSwap(w http.ResponseWriter, events ...string)
func Reswap(w http.ResponseWriter, strategy string)
func Retarget(w http.ResponseWriter, selector string)
func Refresh(w http.ResponseWriter)
func PushURL(w http.ResponseWriter, url string)
func ReplaceURL(w http.ResponseWriter, url string)
```

---

### db

> Package db provides PostgreSQL connection management with retry, keep-alive,
> and schema migration utilities.

#### Types

```go
type OnRetryFunc func(attempt int, err error, backoff time.Duration)

type ConnectConfig struct {
    MaxOpenConns    int
    MaxIdleConns    int
    ConnMaxIdleTime time.Duration
    ConnMaxLifetime time.Duration
    MaxRetries      int
    AttemptTimeout  time.Duration
    PgBouncerSafe   bool
    Logger          *slog.Logger
    OnRetry         OnRetryFunc
}

type ConnectOption func(*ConnectConfig)

type KeepAliveConfig struct {
    Interval time.Duration
    Timeout  time.Duration
}

type MigrateConfig struct {
    FS        embed.FS
    Directory string
    Driver    string
}
```

#### Connection

```go
func Connect(databaseURL string, opts ...ConnectOption) (*sqlx.DB, error)
```
Opens a PostgreSQL connection using context.Background().

```go
func ConnectContext(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string, opts ...ConnectOption) (*sqlx.DB, error)
```
Opens a PostgreSQL connection with retry and exponential backoff + jitter.

#### Connection Options

```go
func WithMaxOpenConns(n int) ConnectOption
func WithMaxIdleConns(n int) ConnectOption
func WithConnMaxIdleTime(d time.Duration) ConnectOption
func WithConnMaxLifetime(d time.Duration) ConnectOption
func WithMaxRetries(n int) ConnectOption
func WithAttemptTimeout(d time.Duration) ConnectOption
func WithPgBouncerSafe(enabled bool) ConnectOption      // forces pgx simple protocol
func WithLogger(l *slog.Logger) ConnectOption
func WithOnRetry(fn OnRetryFunc) ConnectOption
```

#### Keep-Alive

```go
func StartKeepAlive(ctx context.Context, db *sqlx.DB, interval time.Duration, _ int)
```
Backward-compatible keep-alive loop. poolSize parameter is ignored.

```go
func StartKeepAliveWithConfig(ctx context.Context, db *sqlx.DB, cfg KeepAliveConfig)
```
Launches a goroutine that periodically runs PingContext with a timeout.

#### Migration

```go
func Migrate(db *sqlx.DB, cfg MigrateConfig) error          // run all up migrations
func MigrateDown(db *sqlx.DB, cfg MigrateConfig) error      // roll back all
func MigrateSteps(db *sqlx.DB, cfg MigrateConfig, n int) error  // +n up, -n down
func MigrateVersion(db *sqlx.DB, cfg MigrateConfig) (version uint, dirty bool, err error)
func MigrateForce(db *sqlx.DB, cfg MigrateConfig, version int) error  // fix dirty state
```

---

### db/sqlite

> Package sqlite provides SQLite connection management and schema migration
> utilities for hamr-scaffolded projects. It uses the pure-Go
> `modernc.org/sqlite` driver so projects compile without CGO. Mirrors
> `pkg/db`'s API shape with simpler semantics for a local-file database: no
> retries, no pool tuning, safe pragmas by default.
>
> Import path: github.com/FyrmForge/hamr/pkg/db/sqlite

#### Types

```go
type ConnectConfig struct {
    JournalMode  string        // default "WAL"
    ForeignKeys  bool          // default true
    BusyTimeout  time.Duration // default 5s
    MaxOpenConns int           // default 1 (SQLite is single-writer)
}

type ConnectOption func(*ConnectConfig)

type MigrateConfig struct {
    FS        embed.FS
    Directory string
}
```

#### Connection

```go
func Connect(path string, opts ...ConnectOption) (*sqlx.DB, error)
```
Opens a SQLite database file using context.Background(). Pass `:memory:` for
an in-memory DB (handy for tests).

```go
func ConnectContext(ctx context.Context, path string, opts ...ConnectOption) (*sqlx.DB, error)
```
Creates the parent directory if missing, applies the configured pragmas on
every pooled connection, and validates connectivity via PingContext.

#### Connection Options

```go
func WithJournalMode(mode string) ConnectOption  // PRAGMA journal_mode
func WithForeignKeys(enabled bool) ConnectOption // PRAGMA foreign_keys
func WithBusyTimeout(d time.Duration) ConnectOption // PRAGMA busy_timeout
func WithMaxOpenConns(n int) ConnectOption
```

#### Migration

```go
func Migrate(db *sqlx.DB, cfg MigrateConfig) error          // run all up migrations
func MigrateDown(db *sqlx.DB, cfg MigrateConfig) error      // roll back all
func MigrateSteps(db *sqlx.DB, cfg MigrateConfig, n int) error  // +n up, -n down
func MigrateVersion(db *sqlx.DB, cfg MigrateConfig) (version uint, dirty bool, err error)
func MigrateForce(db *sqlx.DB, cfg MigrateConfig, version int) error
```

Wraps golang-migrate's official pure-Go `database/sqlite` driver (distinct
from `database/sqlite3`, which requires CGO via `mattn/go-sqlite3`).

#### SQL Dialect Notes

- Placeholders are `?`, not `$1`/`$2`.
- Prefer `TEXT PRIMARY KEY` or `INTEGER PRIMARY KEY` — no `SERIAL`.
- Use `DATETIME` (`CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` default) instead of `TIMESTAMPTZ`/`NOW()`.
- Booleans are stored as `INTEGER` (0/1).
- Do not wrap migration files in `BEGIN/COMMIT` — golang-migrate runs each
  file in its own transaction.

#### Scaffolded Project Conventions

- Env var: `DATABASE_PATH` (default `./data/<name>.db`), not `DATABASE_URL`.
- `data/` is gitignored; the Dockerfile declares `/data` as a `VOLUME`.
- No Docker Compose is generated when storage is local (nothing needs
  orchestration). S3 storage still emits a compose file for RustFS.
- GORM projects use `github.com/glebarez/sqlite` (pure-Go GORM driver
  wrapping `modernc.org/sqlite`).

---

### middleware

> Package middleware provides HTTP middleware for the HAMR framework.

#### Error Pages

```go
type ErrorPage func(code int, message string) templ.Component

type PageOverride struct {
    Code int
    Page ErrorPage
}

func Page(code int, page ErrorPage) PageOverride
func ErrorPages(defaultPage ErrorPage, overrides ...PageOverride) echo.MiddlewareFunc
```
Per-group middleware that catches errors and renders error pages. Extracts
code/message from `*echo.HTTPError` (default 500), skips committed responses,
logs 5xx via `logging.FromContext`, looks up per-code overrides, and renders
via `respond.HTML`. Place after Logging so Logging sees the correct status.

#### Auth Types

```go
type SubjectLoader func(ctx context.Context, subjectID string) (any, error)

type BrowserAuthOption func(*BrowserAuth)

func NewBrowserAuth(sm *auth.SessionManager, opts ...BrowserAuthOption) *BrowserAuth
func WithSubjectLoader(loader SubjectLoader) BrowserAuthOption
func WithLoginRedirect(url string) BrowserAuthOption   // default: "/login"
func WithHomeRedirect(url string) BrowserAuthOption    // default: "/dashboard"
func WithHXRedirect() BrowserAuthOption                // use HX-Redirect for HTMX requests, 303 for browsers
```

#### Auth Middleware

Auth is split into a loader (DB access, group-level) and policy checks (pure ctx reads, per-route).

```go
func (b *BrowserAuth) Load() echo.MiddlewareFunc
```
Validates session cookie, populates ctx (SubjectIDKey, SessionKey, SubjectKey). The only middleware that touches the DB. Never blocks — no cookie or invalid cookie just continues without auth state.
Usage: `site.Use(auth.Load())`

```go
func (b *BrowserAuth) RequireAuth() echo.MiddlewareFunc
```
Pure ctx check. Redirects to LoginRedirect when no subject in ctx (303 or HX-Redirect).
Usage: `site.GET("/dashboard", handler.Index, auth.RequireAuth())`

```go
func (b *BrowserAuth) RequireNotAuth() echo.MiddlewareFunc
```
Pure ctx check. Redirects to HomeRedirect when subject is in ctx (303 or HX-Redirect).
Usage: `site.GET("/login", loginHandler.Page, auth.RequireNotAuth())`

#### Subject Helpers

```go
func GetSubjectID(c echo.Context) string   // from session or trusted header
func GetSubject(c echo.Context) any        // only when SubjectLoader is configured
func TrustedSubject() echo.MiddlewareFunc  // reads X-Subject-ID with NO verification — internal networks only

// Gated variant: ignores X-Subject-ID unless the request passes the configured
// gate(s); on failure the subject is left unset (downstream RBAC fails closed).
type TrustedSubjectConfig struct {
    SharedSecret   string   // required in SecretHeader (constant-time compared)
    SecretHeader   string   // default: "X-Internal-Secret"
    TrustedProxies []string // c.RealIP() must be within one of these CIDRs
}
func TrustedSubjectWithConfig(cfg TrustedSubjectConfig) echo.MiddlewareFunc
```

#### CSRF

```go
type CSRFConfig struct {
    CookieName  string         // default: "csrf"
    TokenLookup string         // default: "form:csrf_token,header:X-CSRF-Token"
    Secure      bool           // default: true
    SameSite    http.SameSite  // default: Lax
}

func CSRF() echo.MiddlewareFunc
func CSRFWithConfig(cfg CSRFConfig) echo.MiddlewareFunc
```

#### CORS

```go
type CORSConfig struct {
    AllowOrigins     []string
    AllowMethods     []string
    AllowHeaders     []string
    AllowCredentials bool
}

func CORS() echo.MiddlewareFunc
// With no AllowOrigins configured, cross-origin requests are DENIED (no "*").
func CORSWithConfig(cfg CORSConfig) echo.MiddlewareFunc
```

#### Flash

```go
type FlashType string

const (
    FlashInfo    FlashType = "info"
    FlashSuccess FlashType = "success"
    FlashWarning FlashType = "warning"
    FlashError   FlashType = "error"
)

type FlashMessage struct {
    Message string    `json:"message"`
    Type    FlashType `json:"type"`
}

type FlashConfig struct {
    Path   string  // default: "/"
    Secure bool    // default: true
}

const FlashCookieName = "hamr_flash"

func Flash() echo.MiddlewareFunc
func FlashWithConfig(cfg FlashConfig) echo.MiddlewareFunc
func SetFlash(c echo.Context, message string, flashType FlashType)
func GetFlash(c echo.Context) *FlashMessage  // nil if none
```

#### Cache Control

```go
type CacheConfig struct {
    ImmutableExtensions []string  // default: DefaultImmutableExtensions
    ImmutableMaxAge     int       // seconds, default: 31536000 (1 year)
    StaticExtensions    []string  // default: DefaultStaticExtensions
    StaticMaxAge        int       // seconds, default: 86400 (1 day)
    DisableCaching      bool
    AllowDynamicCaching bool      // default false: dynamic responses get "no-store, private"
}

var DefaultImmutableExtensions = []string{".webp", ".jpg", ".jpeg", ".png", ".gif", ".svg", ".ico", ".woff2", ".woff", ".ttf", ".eot"}
var DefaultStaticExtensions    = []string{".css", ".js"}

func CacheControl(disableCaching bool) echo.MiddlewareFunc
func CacheControlWithConfig(cfg CacheConfig) echo.MiddlewareFunc
```
`CacheControl` delegates to `CacheControlWithConfig`. Sets Cache-Control headers
based on asset type. Fingerprinted assets (URLs matching `*.HASH.*` pattern) are
automatically served with immutable headers regardless of extension. When
DisableCaching is true, every response gets no-cache directives.

#### Security Headers

```go
type SecureConfig struct {
    ContentSecurityPolicy string  // default: "default-src 'self'"
    XFrameOptions         string  // default: "DENY"
    ReferrerPolicy        string  // default: "strict-origin-when-cross-origin"
    XSSProtection         string  // default: "0"
    ContentTypeNosniff    string  // default: "nosniff"
}

func Secure() echo.MiddlewareFunc
func SecureWithConfig(cfg SecureConfig) echo.MiddlewareFunc
```
Zero-value fields use defaults. Backwards-compatible — existing code that only sets
`ContentSecurityPolicy` continues to work unchanged.

#### RBAC

RBAC middleware is applied per-route alongside auth middleware:
`site.GET("/admin", handler.Dashboard, requireAuth, requireAdmin)`

```go
type RoleChecker func(subject any, roles []string) bool
type ActiveChecker func(subject any) bool

func RequireRoles(checker RoleChecker, roles ...string) echo.MiddlewareFunc
```
Returns 401 if no subject, 403 if role check fails.

```go
func RequireActive(checker ActiveChecker) echo.MiddlewareFunc
```
Returns 401 if no subject, 403 if account not active.

#### Audit

```go
type AuditEntry struct {
    ActorID    string         `json:"actor_id"`
    Action     string         `json:"action"`
    EntityType string         `json:"entity_type"`
    Data       map[string]any `json:"data"`
    Timestamp  time.Time      `json:"timestamp"`
}

type AuditLogger interface {
    Log(ctx context.Context, entry *AuditEntry) error
}

type AuditConfig struct {
    Logger      AuditLogger
    ActorIDFunc func(c echo.Context) string  // default: GetSubjectID
}

func Audit(logger AuditLogger) echo.MiddlewareFunc
func AuditWithConfig(cfg AuditConfig) echo.MiddlewareFunc
```

#### Rate Limiting

```go
type DB interface {
    ExecContext(ctx context.Context, query string, args ...any) (sql.Result, error)
    QueryRowContext(ctx context.Context, query string, args ...any) *sql.Row
}

type RateLimitStore interface {
    Allow(ctx context.Context, key string, rate int, window time.Duration) (allowed bool, remaining int, resetAt time.Time, err error)
}

type RateLimitConfig struct {
    Store      RateLimitStore
    Rate       int                                   // max requests per window (default: 60)
    Burst      int                                   // additional burst (default: 10)
    Window     time.Duration                         // sliding window (default: 1 minute)
    KeyFunc    func(c echo.Context) (string, error)  // default: c.RealIP()
    FailClosed bool                                  // deny on store errors (default: false)
}

func RateLimit(store RateLimitStore) echo.MiddlewareFunc
func RateLimitWithConfig(cfg RateLimitConfig) echo.MiddlewareFunc
```

##### Memory Store

```go
type MemoryStore struct{ /* unexported */ }
type MemoryStoreOption func(*MemoryStore)

func NewMemoryStore(opts ...MemoryStoreOption) *MemoryStore
func WithMaxSize(n int) MemoryStoreOption
func WithAutoCleanup(ctx context.Context, interval time.Duration) MemoryStoreOption
func WithWindow(d time.Duration) MemoryStoreOption

func (s *MemoryStore) Allow(_ context.Context, key string, rate int, dur time.Duration) (bool, int, time.Time, error)
func (s *MemoryStore) CleanupExpired(window time.Duration)
```

##### PostgreSQL Store

```go
type PGStore struct{ /* unexported */ }

func NewPGStore(db DB) *PGStore
func (s *PGStore) CreateTable(ctx context.Context) error         // creates _rate_limits table
func (s *PGStore) Allow(ctx context.Context, key string, rate int, dur time.Duration) (bool, int, time.Time, error)
func (s *PGStore) Cleanup(ctx context.Context, dur time.Duration) (int64, error)
```

#### Locale

```go
type LocaleConfig struct {
    Bundle         *i18n.Bundle
    CookieName     string                       // default: "hamr_locale"
    CookieMaxAge   int                          // default: 31536000 (1 year)
    CookieSecure   bool
    DefaultLocale  string                       // from bundle if empty
    UserLocaleFunc func(c echo.Context) string  // optional: user preference
}

func LocaleFromPath(cfg LocaleConfig) echo.MiddlewareFunc
```
Pre-router middleware (register with e.Pre()). Extracts locale from first URL
path segment. Redirects to /{defaultLocale}/... if no valid prefix found.

```go
func LocaleFromPreference(cfg LocaleConfig) echo.MiddlewareFunc
```
Resolves locale from: UserLocaleFunc > cookie > Accept-Language > default.

```go
func GetLocale(c echo.Context) string
func GetDirection(c echo.Context) string
```

---

### ptr

> Package ptr provides generic and concrete helpers for working with pointers.

```go
func To[T any](v T) *T                // returns pointer to v
func From[T any](p *T) T              // dereferences, zero value if nil
func FromOr[T any](p *T, def T) T     // dereferences, def if nil

func String(p *string) string          // "" if nil
func Int(p *int) int                   // 0 if nil
func Bool(p *bool) bool               // false if nil

func IntToStr(p *int) string           // "" if nil, else strconv.Itoa
func BoolToYesNo(p *bool) string       // "" if nil, "Yes"/"No"
```

---

## Utility Packages

### janitor

> Package janitor provides a cron-based background task runner with per-task
> schedules, timeouts, and pre/post hooks.

#### Task Interface

```go
type Task interface {
    Name() string
    Run(ctx context.Context) (int64, error)
}
```

#### Hook Types

```go
type PreRunFunc  func(ctx context.Context, taskName string) error
type PostRunFunc func(ctx context.Context, taskName string, affected int64, taskErr error)
type PreTickFunc func(ctx context.Context) error
type PostTickFunc func(ctx context.Context)
```

#### Janitor

```go
type Janitor struct{ /* unexported */ }
type Option func(*Janitor)

func New(opts ...Option) *Janitor
func (j *Janitor) AddTask(schedule string, task Task) *Janitor
func (j *Janitor) Start(ctx context.Context) error
func (j *Janitor) Stop()
```

Schedules support standard cron syntax and robfig/cron descriptors
("@every 5m", "@daily", "@hourly").

#### Options

```go
func WithTimeout(d time.Duration) Option   // per-task timeout (default 30s)
func WithLogger(l *slog.Logger) Option
func WithPreRun(fn PreRunFunc) Option      // runs before each task
func WithPostRun(fn PostRunFunc) Option    // runs after each task
func WithPreTick(fn PreTickFunc) Option    // runs before scheduled execution
func WithPostTick(fn PostTickFunc) Option  // runs after scheduled execution
func WithRunImmediately() Option           // fire all tasks once on Start
```

---

### async

> Package async provides concurrency primitives with panic recovery.

#### Concurrency Functions

```go
func All(ctx context.Context, fns ...func(context.Context) error) error
```
Runs fns concurrently. First error cancels remaining work via derived context.
Panics recovered and converted to errors.

```go
func Settle(ctx context.Context, fns ...func(context.Context) error) []error
```
Runs fns concurrently. Never short-circuits — all functions run to completion.

```go
func Map[T, R any](ctx context.Context, items []T, fn func(context.Context, T) (R, error)) ([]R, error)
```
Applies fn to every item concurrently, returns results in order. First error
cancels remaining.

```go
func Fire(fn func())
```
Spawns a goroutine with panic recovery. Panics logged to slog.Default().

#### Group

```go
type Group struct{ /* unexported */ }
type GroupOption func(*Group)

func NewGroup(opts ...GroupOption) *Group
func (g *Group) Go(fn func())    // spawns work; blocks at limit; no-op after Close
func (g *Group) Close()          // waits for all in-flight goroutines

func WithGroupLogger(l *slog.Logger) GroupOption
func WithLimit(n int) GroupOption  // caps concurrent goroutines (clamped to >= 1)
func WithMetrics(m GroupMetrics) GroupOption

// Warning: callbacks run synchronously on the Group hot path and during worker
// teardown. Implementations must not block, do slow I/O, or panic.
// Every Dispatched is followed by exactly one Completed or Panicked.
// No callbacks fire for calls to Go after Close.
type GroupMetrics interface {
    Blocked()                            // semaphore contention occurred (fired after the wait completes)
    Dispatched(blocked time.Duration)    // job accepted; worker goroutine about to spawn (blocked=0 if no wait)
    Completed(duration time.Duration)    // job finished without panic recovery
    Panicked(duration time.Duration)     // job finished via panic recovery
}
```

---

### templint

> Package templint provides a static linter for .templ files.
> Checks for inline control flow (silently dropped by templ), missing
> accessibility attributes, and style anti-patterns.

#### Types

```go
type Severity int

const (
    Warning Severity = iota
    Error
)

func (s Severity) String() string
func ParseSeverity(s string) (Severity, error)

type Diagnostic struct {
    File     string
    Line     int
    Col      int
    Rule     string
    Severity Severity
    Message  string
}

func (d Diagnostic) String() string  // "file:line:col: severity[rule] message"

type Rule interface {
    ID() string
    Check(filename string, lines []string) []Diagnostic
}
```

#### Configuration

```go
type Config struct {
    Rules map[string]Severity  // rule ID -> severity to report at; rules absent from the map are disabled
}

func LoadConfig(path string) (*Config, error)
// Reads [lint.templ] from hamr.toml as a flat `rule = "warning"|"error"|"off"` map.
// Returns nil if the file is missing or [lint.templ] is empty.
// Unknown rule IDs and invalid severities return an error.

func AllRuleIDs() []string                     // sorted IDs of every known rule
func DefaultSeverity(id string) Severity       // recommended default; Warning for unknown IDs
```

```toml
# Example [lint.templ] block. Rules omitted from this section are disabled;
# "off" is equivalent to omitting the rule.
[lint.templ]
inline-if              = "error"
no-native-form-actions = "error"
img-alt                = "warning"
inline-style           = "off"
```

#### Linter

```go
type Linter struct{ /* unexported */ }

func New(cfg *Config) *Linter  // nil cfg or empty Rules = nothing runs; only listed rules are enabled
func (l *Linter) LintFile(path string) ([]Diagnostic, error)
func (l *Linter) LintDir(dir string) ([]Diagnostic, error)

func FilterBySeverity(diags []Diagnostic, minSev Severity) []Diagnostic
func HasErrors(diags []Diagnostic) bool
```

#### Built-in Rules

| ID | Default Severity | Detects |
|---|---|---|
| `inline-if` | Error | `if cond { <html>` on same line (silently dropped by templ) |
| `inline-for` | Error | `for expr { <html>` on same line |
| `inline-switch` | Error | `switch expr { <html>` on same line |
| `no-native-form-actions` | Error | `action=`, `method=`, or `formaction=` attribute on any element — use `hx-*` instead |
| `htmx-conflict` | Error | Same element has both an `hx-*` attribute and a native form attribute |
| `img-alt` | Warning | `<img>` tags missing `alt` attribute |
| `no-href` | Warning | `<a>` tags missing `href` attribute |
| `inline-style` | Warning | `style="..."` or `style={` attributes |
| `empty-class` | Warning | `class=""` attributes |
| `js-href` | Warning | `href="javascript:..."` |

---

## Optional Packages

### e2e

> Package e2e provides reusable go-rod browser helpers for E2E testing.
> Projects import this package in their e2e/ test files.

#### Config & Options

```go
type Config struct {
    Headless            bool          // default: true,  env: E2E_HEADLESS
    NoSandbox           bool          // default: true,  env: E2E_NO_SANDBOX
    SlowMotion          time.Duration // default: 0,     env: E2E_SLOW_MOTION
    Timeout             time.Duration // default: 10s,   env: E2E_TIMEOUT
    ArtifactDir         string        // default: "testdata/e2e-artifacts", env: E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR
    ScreenshotOnFailure bool          // default: true,  env: E2E_SCREENSHOT_ON_FAIL
    HTMLDumpOnFailure   bool          // default: true,  env: E2E_HTML_DUMP_ON_FAIL
}

type Option func(*Config)

func WithHeadless(b bool) Option
func WithSlowMotion(d time.Duration) Option
func WithTimeout(d time.Duration) Option
func WithArtifactDir(path string) Option
func WithScreenshotOnFailure(b bool) Option
func WithHTMLDumpOnFailure(b bool) Option
func WithNoSandbox(b bool) Option
```

#### Browser Lifecycle

```go
func SetupBrowser(t *testing.T, opts ...Option) *rod.Browser
```
Launches Chromium configured via opts and env vars. Registers t.Cleanup to
close browser.

```go
func NewPage(t *testing.T, browser *rod.Browser, url string) *rod.Page
```
Creates a tab, navigates to url, waits for load. Captures screenshot/HTML on
failure. Closed via t.Cleanup.

#### Element Actions

```go
func Input(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector, value string)      // clear + type
func Click(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector string)
func SelectOption(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector, value string)
```

#### Element Queries

```go
func ElementExists(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector string) bool
func ElementNotExists(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector string) bool
func ElementText(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector string) string
func ElementAttribute(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector, attr string) string
func ElementCount(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector string) int
```

#### Waiters

```go
func WaitForElement(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector string, timeout time.Duration) *rod.Element
func WaitForURLChange(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, currentURL string, timeout time.Duration)
func WaitForElementRemoved(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector string, timeout time.Duration)
```

#### HTMX Helpers

```go
func WaitForHTMXIdle(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, timeout time.Duration)
```
Waits until no elements carry the htmx-request class.

```go
func WaitForHTMXSwap(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector string, timeout time.Duration)
```
Waits until the HTML of selector changes (indicating an HTMX swap).

```go
func ClickAndWaitHTMX(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector string, timeout time.Duration)
```
Clicks selector and waits for all HTMX requests to settle.

#### Assertions

```go
func AssertElementExists(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector string)
func AssertElementNotExists(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector string)
func AssertElementNotVisible(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector string)
func AssertElementContainsText(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector, text string)
func AssertElementHasClass(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector, class string)
func AssertElementCount(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, selector string, expected int)
func AssertURL(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, expected string)
func AssertURLContains(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, substring string)
```

#### Artifacts

```go
func SaveScreenshot(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, name string)  // logs warning on failure
func SavePageHTML(t *testing.T, page *rod.Page, name string)    // logs warning on failure
```

---

### auth

> Package auth provides password hashing with Argon2id and
> cryptographically-secure token generation.

#### Password Hashing

```go
type HashConfig struct {
    Time        uint32
    Memory      uint32
    Parallelism uint8
    KeyLength   uint32
    SaltLength  uint32
}

var DefaultHashConfig = HashConfig{
    Time: 3, Memory: 64 * 1024, Parallelism: 2,
    KeyLength: 32, SaltLength: 16,
}

func HashPassword(password string) (string, error)
```
Hashes using DefaultHashConfig. Returns PHC-format encoded string.

```go
func HashPasswordWithConfig(password string, cfg HashConfig) (string, error)
```

```go
func CheckPassword(password, encodedHash string) (bool, error)
```
(true, nil) on match, (false, nil) on mismatch, error if hash cannot be parsed.

```go
func NeedsRehash(encodedHash string) (bool, error)
```
Reports whether hash was created with different parameters than DefaultHashConfig.

#### Token Generation

```go
func GenerateToken() (string, error)      // 32-byte, base64 raw-URL encoded
func GenerateTokenN(n int) (string, error) // n-byte variant
```

#### Session Types

```go
type Session struct {
    ID        string
    SubjectID string
    Token     string
    ExpiresAt time.Time
    CreatedAt time.Time
    // unexported: metadata any
}

func (s *Session) SetMetadata(v any)       // for SessionStore implementations
func (s *Session) RawMetadata() any
func SessionMetadata[T any](s *Session) (T, error)  // type-safe extraction
```

#### Session Store Interface

```go
type SessionStore interface {
    Create(ctx context.Context, s *Session) error
    GetByToken(ctx context.Context, token string) (*Session, error)  // (nil, nil) = not found
    Delete(ctx context.Context, id string) error
    DeleteBySubjectID(ctx context.Context, subjectID string) error
}

type SessionToucher interface {
    Touch(ctx context.Context, id string, newExpiresAt time.Time) error
}
```

#### Session Manager

```go
type SessionManager struct{ /* unexported */ }
type SessionOption func(*SessionManager)

func NewSessionManager(store SessionStore, opts ...SessionOption) *SessionManager
```
Defaults: 7-day duration, cookie "session_token", path "/", Secure, SameSiteLax.

```go
func (m *SessionManager) CreateSession(ctx context.Context, subjectID string, metadata any) (*Session, error)
func (m *SessionManager) ValidateSession(ctx context.Context, token string) (*Session, error)
func (m *SessionManager) DeleteSession(ctx context.Context, id string) error
func (m *SessionManager) DeleteSubjectSessions(ctx context.Context, subjectID string) error

func (m *SessionManager) CookieName() string
func (m *SessionManager) CookiePath() string
func (m *SessionManager) CookieDomain() string
func (m *SessionManager) CookieSecure() bool
func (m *SessionManager) SameSite() http.SameSite
func (m *SessionManager) Duration() time.Duration
```

#### Session Options

```go
func WithDuration(d time.Duration) SessionOption
func WithCookieName(name string) SessionOption
func WithCookiePath(path string) SessionOption
func WithCookieDomain(domain string) SessionOption
func WithCookieSecure(secure bool) SessionOption
func WithSameSite(ss http.SameSite) SessionOption
func WithSlidingRefresh(threshold time.Duration) SessionOption
```
WithSlidingRefresh: when session age exceeds threshold, type-asserts store to
SessionToucher and calls Touch to extend expiry.

---

### i18n

> Package i18n provides internationalisation support: JSON translation files,
> CLDR plural rules, text/template interpolation, and a Bundle that holds
> per-locale Translators.

#### Plural Types

```go
type PluralCategory string

const (
    Zero  PluralCategory = "zero"
    One   PluralCategory = "one"
    Two   PluralCategory = "two"
    Few   PluralCategory = "few"
    Many  PluralCategory = "many"
    Other PluralCategory = "other"
)

type PluralRule func(n int) PluralCategory
```

#### Plural Functions

```go
func RegisterRule(lang string, rule PluralRule)
func RuleFor(lang string) PluralRule
```
Tries full tag (e.g. "fr-CA"), then base language ("fr"), then custom rules,
then builtins, falling back to English.

#### Direction

```go
func DirectionFor(lang string) string  // "rtl" for RTL languages, "ltr" otherwise
```

#### JSON Helpers

```go
func IsPluralObject(obj map[string]any) bool
func InterpolationVars(s string) []string  // extracts {{.VarName}} placeholders
```

#### Validation

```go
type ValidationError struct {
    Locale  string
    Key     string
    Message string
}

func (e ValidationError) Error() string  // "[locale] key: message"
```

#### Bundle

```go
type BundleConfig struct {
    LocaleDir         string       // directory containing *.json files
    DefaultLocale     string       // e.g. "en"
    FallbackToDefault *bool        // default true; nil = true
    Logger            *slog.Logger // missing/extra-key warnings; nil = slog.Default()
}

type Bundle struct{ /* unexported */ }

func NewBundle(cfg BundleConfig) (*Bundle, error)
func (b *Bundle) Translator(locale string) *Translator
func (b *Bundle) SupportedLocales() []string
func (b *Bundle) HasLocale(locale string) bool
func (b *Bundle) ResolveLocale(code string) (string, bool)  // handles region tags
func (b *Bundle) DefaultLocale() string
```

#### Translator

```go
type Translator struct{ /* unexported */ }

func (t *Translator) Locale() string
func (t *Translator) Direction() string   // "ltr" or "rtl"
func (t *Translator) Has(key string) bool
func (t *Translator) T(key string, args ...any) string
```
T argument patterns:
- No args: return plain string
- int arg: use as plural count; remaining args for interpolation
- map[string]any arg: use as interpolation data
Falls back to fallback translator, then returns key as-is.

#### Context Helpers

```go
func FromContext(c echo.Context) *Translator   // panics if not set
func LocaleFromContext(c echo.Context) string
func DirectionFromContext(c echo.Context) string
```

---

### storage

> Package storage provides a pluggable file storage abstraction with local
> filesystem and S3-compatible backends (AWS S3, RustFS, Cloudflare R2).

#### Interfaces

```go
type FileStorage interface {
    Save(ctx context.Context, path string, r io.Reader) error
    Open(ctx context.Context, path string) (io.ReadCloser, error)
    Delete(ctx context.Context, path string) error
    Exists(ctx context.Context, path string) (bool, error)
    List(ctx context.Context, prefix string) ([]string, error)
}

type SignableStorage interface {
    FileStorage
    SignURL(ctx context.Context, path string, expiry time.Duration) (string, error)
}
```
Save overwrites existing files. Delete is idempotent. List returns all file
paths under the given prefix.

#### Local Storage

```go
type LocalStorage struct{ /* unexported */ }
type LocalOption func(*LocalStorage)

func NewLocalStorage(basePath string, opts ...LocalOption) (*LocalStorage, error)
func WithLocalLogger(l *slog.Logger) LocalOption
```
Methods: Save, Open, Delete, Exists, List (implementing FileStorage).

#### S3 Storage

```go
type S3Config struct {
    Endpoint       string  // e.g. "http://localhost:9000" for RustFS
    Bucket         string
    Region         string
    AccessKeyID    string
    SecretAccessKey string
    UsePathStyle   bool    // true for RustFS / path-style addressing
}

type S3Storage struct{ /* unexported */ }
type S3Option func(*S3Storage)

func NewS3Storage(cfg S3Config, opts ...S3Option) (*S3Storage, error)
func WithS3Logger(l *slog.Logger) S3Option
func WithPublicRead(v bool) S3Option  // applies public-read policy on EnsureBucket
func WithMaxUploadBuffer(n int64) S3Option  // cap for buffering non-seekable Save bodies (default 64 MiB)

func (s *S3Storage) EnsureBucket(ctx context.Context) error
```
Methods: Save, Open, Delete, Exists, List, SignURL (implementing SignableStorage).
`Save` streams seekable bodies straight to S3; a non-seekable body (e.g. an `Open` result piped into `Save`) is buffered into memory first because the AWS signer needs a seekable body to hash it. The buffer is bounded by `WithMaxUploadBuffer` (default 64 MiB) — a larger non-seekable body fails the upload rather than allocating unbounded memory.

---

### media

> Package media provides high-level image and video upload, processing, and
> serving on top of the storage package.

#### Constants

```go
const (
    KB int64 = 1024
    MB int64 = 1024 * 1024
    GB int64 = 1024 * 1024 * 1024
)

const (
    FormatWebP = "webp"
    FormatJPEG = "jpeg"
    FormatPNG  = "png"
)

const (
    TypeImage = "image"
    TypeVideo = "video"
)
```

#### Errors

```go
var (
    ErrFileTooLarge   = errors.New("media: file too large")
    ErrUnknownType    = errors.New("media: unsupported file type")
    ErrVideoTooLong   = errors.New("media: video exceeds maximum duration")
    ErrFFmpegNotFound = errors.New("media: ffmpeg not found in PATH")
    // *FromReaderWithID variants
    ErrInvalidID = errors.New("media: invalid id (must be a canonical UUID)")
    ErrIDExists  = errors.New("media: id already exists")
)
```

#### Types

```go
type ImageSize struct {
    Name   string
    Width  int
    Height int
}

type ImageStoreConfig struct {
    Category    string
    Sizes       []ImageSize
    Quality     int
    Format      string
    MaxSize     int64
    SignedExpiry time.Duration
    BaseURL     string
}

type VideoStoreConfig struct {
    Category          string
    MaxSize           int64
    MaxDuration       float64
    GenerateThumbnail bool
    ThumbnailWidth    int
    BaseURL           string
    SignedExpiry       time.Duration
    Transcode         VideoTranscodeOptions
}

// Activation by population: zero-valued struct = no transcode (save raw
// bytes); any non-zero field flips the store into "re-encode every
// upload to H.264/AAC MP4 with +faststart, -pix_fmt yuv420p,
// -profile:v high" mode. validate() fills in defaults — CRF=23,
// Preset="medium", AudioBitrate="128k", MaxWidth/MaxHeight=0 (no clamp).
// MaxSize is checked against the input bytes (pre-transcode); thumbnail
// still extracted from raw upload. Note: setting only MaxWidth/MaxHeight
// also activates the full re-encode — there is no "clamp size, don't
// re-encode" mode.
type VideoTranscodeOptions struct {
    CRF          int    // effective 1..51, default 23; CRF=0 is treated as "use default", NOT lossless
    Preset       string // x264 preset; default "medium"
    AudioBitrate string // ffmpeg -b:a, default "128k"
    MaxWidth     int    // 0 = no clamp (preserve source)
    MaxHeight    int    // 0 = no clamp (preserve source)
}

func (o VideoTranscodeOptions) IsZero() bool

type Option func(*options)
```

#### Preset Size Sets

```go
var SizesAvatar = []ImageSize{
    {Name: "thumb", Width: 64, Height: 64},
    {Name: "small", Width: 150, Height: 150},
    {Name: "medium", Width: 400, Height: 400},
}

var SizesCard = []ImageSize{
    {Name: "thumb", Width: 64, Height: 64},
    {Name: "small", Width: 150, Height: 150},
    {Name: "medium", Width: 400, Height: 400},
    {Name: "large", Width: 800, Height: 800},
    {Name: "xlarge", Width: 1200, Height: 1200},
}

var SizesIcon = []ImageSize{
    {Name: "small", Width: 100, Height: 100},
    {Name: "medium", Width: 200, Height: 200},
    {Name: "large", Width: 400, Height: 400},
}

var SizeOriginal = []ImageSize{
    {Name: "original", Width: 0, Height: 0},
}
```

#### Detection

```go
func DetectType(fh *multipart.FileHeader) (mediaType string, mimeType string, err error)
```

#### Media Option

```go
func WithLogger(l *slog.Logger) Option
```

#### Image Store

```go
type ImageStore struct{ /* unexported */ }

func NewLocalImageStore(store *storage.LocalStorage, urlPrefix string, cfg ImageStoreConfig, opts ...Option) (*ImageStore, error)
func NewS3ImageStore(store *storage.S3Storage, cfg ImageStoreConfig, opts ...Option) (*ImageStore, error)

func (s *ImageStore) Upload(ctx context.Context, fh *multipart.FileHeader) (*ImageUploadResult, error)
func (s *ImageStore) UploadFromReader(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, size int64) (*ImageUploadResult, error)
func (s *ImageStore) UploadFromReaderWithID(ctx context.Context, id string, r io.Reader, size int64, overwrite bool) (*ImageUploadResult, error)
func (s *ImageStore) Delete(ctx context.Context, id string) error
func (s *ImageStore) GetMedia(id string) ImageRef
func (s *ImageStore) GetMediaCtx(ctx context.Context, id string) ImageRef  // may use signed URLs
func (s *ImageStore) SignedURL(ctx context.Context, path string, expiry time.Duration) (string, error)
func (s *ImageStore) ServeHandler() echo.HandlerFunc
```

`UploadFromReaderWithID` stores bytes under the caller-supplied id instead of generating one. The id MUST be the **36-char canonical UUID form**; hyphenless 32-char, urn:uuid:..., {...} braced, and the empty string are all rejected with `ErrInvalidID` (canonical-only avoids silent bifurcation where the same logical UUID lands at two different storage keys). With `overwrite=false`, returns `ErrIDExists` if the prefix is already populated; pass `overwrite=true` for retry-after-failure paths. `ErrIDExists` is a best-effort precheck — concurrent `*WithID` callers can race on Save, and a losing call's `cleanupPartial` can erase a concurrent winner's writes; use DB-level unique constraints for hard exclusion. On `ErrFileTooLarge` (size pre-check), `ErrInvalidID`, and `ErrIDExists` the reader is not consumed (rejection happens before any read). Best-effort cleanup of any partial writes on error.

#### Image Upload Result

```go
type ImageUploadResult struct {
    ID        string
    MediaType string
    MimeType  string
}

func (r *ImageUploadResult) Path(size string) string
```

#### Image Ref

```go
type ImageRef struct{ /* unexported */ }

func (r ImageRef) Size(name string) string   // URL for named size variant
func (r ImageRef) Thumb() string             // "thumb" size or smallest
func (r ImageRef) Smallest() string          // smallest by width
func (r ImageRef) Biggest() string           // largest by width
```

#### Video Store

```go
type VideoStore struct{ /* unexported */ }

func NewLocalVideoStore(store *storage.LocalStorage, urlPrefix string, cfg VideoStoreConfig, opts ...Option) (*VideoStore, error)
func NewS3VideoStore(store *storage.S3Storage, cfg VideoStoreConfig, opts ...Option) (*VideoStore, error)

func (s *VideoStore) Upload(ctx context.Context, fh *multipart.FileHeader) (*VideoUploadResult, error)
func (s *VideoStore) UploadFromReader(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, size int64) (*VideoUploadResult, error)
func (s *VideoStore) UploadFromReaderWithID(ctx context.Context, id string, r io.Reader, size int64, overwrite bool) (*VideoUploadResult, error)
func (s *VideoStore) Delete(ctx context.Context, id string) error
func (s *VideoStore) GetMedia(id string) VideoRef
func (s *VideoStore) GetMediaCtx(ctx context.Context, id string) VideoRef
func (s *VideoStore) SignedURL(ctx context.Context, path string, expiry time.Duration) (string, error)
func (s *VideoStore) ServeHandler() echo.HandlerFunc
```

`UploadFromReaderWithID` follows the same UUID-validation and existence-check rules as the image variant (strict 36-char canonical only; `ErrIDExists` is best-effort). Thumbnail generation/save failures are logged and ignored — the video bytes are stored regardless, and `result.ThumbnailPath` stays empty when this branch fails.

When `VideoStoreConfig.Transcode` is populated, every `Upload*` call re-encodes the upload to H.264/AAC MP4 (with `+faststart` so the moov atom is at the front for progressive streaming, `-pix_fmt yuv420p` and `-profile:v high` for browser/QuickTime/smart-TV compatibility) before persisting. `result.FileSize` reflects whatever was actually saved — encoded bytes when transcoding, raw bytes otherwise. `result.MimeType` reports `video/mp4` when transcoding ran (regardless of input container) and the source MIME otherwise. The transcode pass is slow (tens of seconds for a one-minute clip on `medium` preset); call `Upload*` from a worker pool such as `pkg/async.Group` rather than from the request goroutine. Internally, transcoding writes to an `os.TempDir()` file and reads it back rather than streaming through stdout, because `+faststart` requires a seekable output. The scale filter uses a chained `scale=...,scale=trunc(iw/2)*2:trunc(ih/2)*2` pattern to satisfy libx264's even-dimension requirement on all ffmpeg versions, sidestepping `force_divisible_by` which only landed in ffmpeg 4.4.

#### Video Upload Result

```go
type VideoUploadResult struct {
    ID            string
    MediaType     string
    MimeType      string
    Duration      float64
    FileSize      int64
    ThumbnailPath string
}

func (r *VideoUploadResult) Path() string
```

#### Video Ref

```go
type VideoRef struct{ /* unexported */ }

func (r VideoRef) Video() string        // video file URL
func (r VideoRef) Thumbnail() string    // thumbnail URL (empty if not generated)
```

---

### websocket

> WebSocket hub with session, subject, and room routing.
> Pure mutex design with no background event-loop goroutine.

#### Event Types

```go
type EventType string

type Event struct {
    Type    EventType       `json:"type"`
    Payload json.RawMessage `json:"payload,omitempty"`
    Target  string          `json:"target,omitempty"`   // CSS selector
    Swap    string          `json:"swap,omitempty"`     // "innerHTML" or "outerHTML"
    HTML    string          `json:"html,omitempty"`     // rendered HTML to swap in
    Trigger string          `json:"trigger,omitempty"`  // HTMX event name
}
```
Three delivery modes:
1. HTML Direct: set Target + HTML, client swaps HTML into target
2. HTMX Trigger: set Target + Trigger, client calls htmx.trigger()
3. Data Only: set Payload only, client handles via registered callback

#### Event Constructors

```go
func NewEvent(eventType EventType, payload any) *Event
func NewHTMLEvent(eventType EventType, target, html string) *Event
func NewOuterHTMLEvent(eventType EventType, target, html string) *Event
func NewTriggerEvent(eventType EventType, target, trigger string) *Event
func (e *Event) JSON() []byte
```

#### Client

```go
type Client struct {
    SessionID string
    SubjectID string
    Rooms     map[string]bool
    Meta      map[string]any
}
```

#### Hub

```go
type Hub struct{ /* unexported */ }
type HubOption func(*Hub)

type HubStats struct {
    Clients  int
    Subjects int
    Rooms    int
}

func NewHub(opts ...HubOption) *Hub
func (h *Hub) Handler() echo.HandlerFunc     // upgrades HTTP to WebSocket
func (h *Hub) Close()                        // cancels context, waits for pumps
func (h *Hub) Stats() HubStats
```

#### Hub Options

```go
func WithSessionIDFunc(fn func(r *http.Request) string) HubOption
func WithSubjectIDFunc(fn func(r *http.Request) string) HubOption
func WithOnMessage(fn func(*Client, []byte)) HubOption       // nil = server-push-only
func WithAcceptOptions(opts *ws.AcceptOptions) HubOption
func WithLogger(l *slog.Logger) HubOption
func WithSendBufferSize(n int) HubOption                     // default: 256, <=0 uses default
```

#### Hub Sending

```go
func (h *Hub) SendToSession(sessionID string, msg []byte) bool
func (h *Hub) SendToSubject(subjectID string, msg []byte) int   // returns drop count
func (h *Hub) SendToRoom(room string, msg []byte) int
func (h *Hub) SendToRoomExcept(room string, msg []byte, exceptSessionID string) int
func (h *Hub) Broadcast(msg []byte) int
```

#### Hub Room/Subject Management

```go
func (h *Hub) JoinRoom(c *Client, room string)
func (h *Hub) LeaveRoom(c *Client, room string)
func (h *Hub) AssociateSubject(sessionID, subjectID string)
```

#### Emitter

```go
type Emitter struct{ /* unexported */ }

func NewEmitter(hub *Hub) *Emitter
func (e *Emitter) ToSession(sessionID string, event *Event)
func (e *Emitter) ToSubject(subjectID string, event *Event)
func (e *Emitter) ToRoom(room string, event *Event)
func (e *Emitter) ToRoomExcept(room string, event *Event, exceptSessionID string)
func (e *Emitter) Broadcast(event *Event)
```

---

### fingerprint

> Package fingerprint provides content-based static asset fingerprinting.
> Hashes file contents (SHA-256, 12 hex chars), writes fingerprinted copies
> to a separate dist directory, and generates a Go source file with the
> manifest baked in at compile time.

```go
func Fingerprint(srcDir, distDir string) (*Manifest, error)
```
Walks srcDir, hashes each file, writes fingerprinted copies to distDir (mirroring
directory structure). Wipes distDir first. Validates distDir is safe to wipe
(not `.`, not srcDir, must contain sentinel or be empty). Skips `.gitkeep`.

```go
func Clean(distDir string) error
```
Removes the dist directory entirely. Same safety validation as `Fingerprint`.

```go
func IsFingerprinted(path string) bool
```
Reports whether the URL path contains a fingerprint hash (`*.HASH.*` pattern).

```go
type Manifest struct {
    Files map[string]string
}
func (m *Manifest) WriteGoManifest(path, pkg string) error
```
`WriteGoManifest` generates a Go source file with the manifest baked in as a
compiled `map[string]string` literal. No runtime loading needed.

CLI: `hamr gen static [--clean]`, config via `[static]` in hamr.toml

---

### sync

> Package sync provides file-watching and S3-syncing for static assets.

```go
func SyncAll(ctx context.Context, store storage.FileStorage, dir string) error
```
One-shot upload of all files under dir. Creates bucket if store implements
BucketEnsurer.

```go
func WatchAndSync(ctx context.Context, store storage.FileStorage, dir string) error
```
Watches dir for filesystem changes and syncs to storage. Blocks until ctx
cancelled.

```go
func Key(dir, path string) string
```
Converts filesystem path to S3 key relative to dir. Returns "" for skippable
files (e.g. .gitkeep).

```go
type BucketEnsurer interface {
    EnsureBucket(ctx context.Context) error
}
```

---

### stripemock (local Stripe backend, hosted by `hamr dev`)

> A local Stripe-compatible HTTP backend that the official `stripe-go` SDK
> talks to in development. App code uses real `stripe-go` calls in both dev
> and production — the only difference is whether `STRIPE_MOCK=true` is
> set, which makes the scaffolded `main.go` point `stripe-go` at the proxy
> URL instead of api.stripe.com. The previous `pkg/stripemock` package has
> been **removed**; the mock lives entirely server-side in
> `internal/devserver/stripemock*.go`.

**Architecture.** Both the Stripe API surface (`/v1/*`) and the dev UI
(`/__hamr/stripe/*`) mount on hamr's single proxy mux (default port
`:3000`). `stripe-go` requires `req.URL.Path` to start with `/v1` — that's
satisfied as long as paths begin with `/v1`, regardless of the listener.
Earlier hamr versions used a dedicated `:3001` listener for the API; the
single-mux model removes that port and the `[dev.stripe].api_listen`
config knob. `http.ServeMux`'s longest-prefix matching keeps `/v1/*` and
`/__hamr/*` from colliding with the catch-all `/` reverse-proxy to the
app. **Caveat:** apps that version their own REST API at `/v1/*` (e.g.
their own `/v1/accounts`, `/v1/refunds`, `/v1/payouts`) and turn on
`[dev.stripe].enabled` will have those exact paths intercepted by the
mock — the mock can't tell which `/v1/*` paths are Stripe's vs theirs.
Workaround: serve the app's API under a different prefix (most projects
already use `/api/v1/*`).

**URL derivation.** `proxyClientBaseURL(cfg)` (in `stripemock.go`) builds
`http://<normalized-host>:<port>` from `proxy.listen` and is used for the
mock's `BaseURL` (rendered into checkout-session URLs) plus the
`HAMR_DEV_URL` and `HAMR_STRIPE_MOCK_URL` env vars hamr injects into the
spawned app. `normalizeHost` rewrites bind-only forms (`:port`,
`0.0.0.0:port`, `[::]:port`) to `localhost:port` so the URL is
client-reachable. Config validation rejects port `:0` (random-bind) plus
absent `[proxy]` when either mock is enabled — the URL must be derivable
from config alone, not from the runtime listener.

**API surface (checkout sessions).**
- `POST /v1/checkout/sessions` — create. Decodes Stripe's bracket-form body
  (`line_items[0][price_data][currency]=gbp` etc.) into a stored session.
  Returns a `checkout.session`-shaped JSON response with `URL` pointing at
  `/__hamr/stripe/checkout?session=<id>` on the proxy.
- `GET /v1/checkout/sessions/{id}` — retrieve. Same JSON shape.

**API surface (Connect accounts).**
- `POST /v1/accounts` — create connected account. Defaults: type=express,
  country=US, default_currency derived from country (GB→gbp, EU/DE/FR/IT/ES/NL→eur,
  JP→jpy, otherwise usd). New accounts start in pre-onboarding state:
  `charges_enabled=false`, `payouts_enabled=false`, `details_submitted=false`,
  `requirements.currently_due=[business_type, external_account, tos_acceptance.date,
  tos_acceptance.ip]`, `requirements.disabled_reason="requirements.past_due"`.
- `GET /v1/accounts/{id}` — retrieve. Returns the same shape with current state.
- `POST /v1/account_links` — generate hosted onboarding URL pointing at
  `/__hamr/stripe/onboarding?account=<id>` on the proxy. `expires_at` set
  to mirror Stripe's 5-minute TTL but not enforced.

**Dev UI (account onboarding).**
- `GET /__hamr/stripe/onboarding?account=<id>` — renders the account state
  (type, country, currency, enabled flags, requirements list) and a
  Complete button. Already-onboarded accounts show a green banner instead.
- `POST /__hamr/stripe/account/complete` — flips `charges_enabled`,
  `payouts_enabled`, `details_submitted` to true, clears `currently_due`
  and `disabled_reason`, fires `account.updated`. 409 Conflict on
  double-submit. Same-origin guard.

**API surface (PaymentIntents — Connect-aware).**
- `POST /v1/payment_intents` — create. Reads the `Stripe-Account` request
  header (direct-charge model) and the body fields
  `application_fee_amount`, `transfer_data[destination]`,
  `transfer_data[amount]`, `on_behalf_of`, plus standard PI fields
  (`amount`, `currency`, `capture_method`, `confirmation_method`,
  `description`, `statement_descriptor`, `receipt_email`, `customer`,
  `payment_method`, `metadata`). New PIs without a payment_method start in
  `requires_payment_method`; with one, `requires_confirmation`.
  **Validation**: `currency` required, `amount > 0`,
  `application_fee_amount` non-negative and ≤ amount,
  `transfer_data.destination` (if provided) must reference an existing
  connected account.
- `GET /v1/payment_intents/{id}` — retrieve. `latest_charge` is rendered as
  an inline charge object once the outcome runs (caller doesn't need a
  separate charge.Get round-trip).
- `POST /v1/payment_intents/{id}/confirm` — advance from
  `requires_payment_method` to `processing` for auto-capture (mirrors real
  Stripe's sync card flow), or to `requires_capture` if
  `capture_method=manual`. Defaults the payment_method to `pm_card_visa`
  if absent so the rest of the flow doesn't error.
- `POST /v1/payment_intents/{id}/capture` — capture a `requires_capture`
  (manual-capture) PI: creates the Charge, advances to `succeeded`, fires
  `payment_intent.succeeded` + `charge.succeeded` (+ `transfer.created` for
  destination charges). Partial capture via `amount_to_capture`. While in
  `requires_capture` the serialized PI reports `amount_capturable` (the
  authorized amount); after capture `amount_received` is the captured amount.

**Dev UI (PaymentIntent).**
- `GET /__hamr/stripe/payment_intent?id=<pi_id>` — renders the amount,
  application fee, transfer destination + transfer amount (for destination
  charges), an "as <acct>" tag (for direct charges), and Succeed/Fail
  buttons. Already-terminal PIs return 410 Gone.
- `POST /__hamr/stripe/payment_intent/complete` — outcomes: `succeed` or
  `fail`.

**Cascade on PaymentIntent succeed (destination charge).**
Three signed webhooks fire **in order** from a single goroutine, matching
real Stripe's delivery order:
1. `payment_intent.succeeded` — PI status now `succeeded`,
   `amount_received = amount`, `latest_charge` populated.
2. `charge.succeeded` — auto-created Charge with
   `paid=true`, `captured=true`, `application_fee_amount`,
   `destination` (the connected account), `transfer` (the auto-created
   transfer ID), `payment_intent` (this PI).
3. `transfer.created` — auto-created Transfer to the connected account.
   `amount = pi.amount - application_fee_amount` by default; overridden
   when `transfer_data.amount` was set explicitly. `source_transaction`
   points at the Charge.

**Cascade on PaymentIntent succeed (no destination).** Just events 1 + 2;
no transfer is created.

**Cascade on PaymentIntent fail.** Only `payment_intent.payment_failed`.
No Charge is synthesised — mirrors real Stripe's sync card-decline path
where no Charge object exists for a payment that never settled.

**Connect models supported.** Both:
- **Destination charge** — platform creates PI on its own account, sets
  `transfer_data[destination]` to forward funds (minus fee) to the
  connected account on success. Platform is the merchant of record.
- **Direct charge** — platform creates PI AS the connected account via
  the `Stripe-Account` header. Connected account is the merchant of
  record; platform takes its cut via `application_fee_amount`.

**API surface (Refunds — Connect-aware).**
- `POST /v1/refunds` — sync-success model that matches real Stripe's card
  refund behavior: status is `succeeded` immediately, the
  `charge.refunded` webhook fires async. Caller passes **exactly one** of
  `payment_intent` or `charge` (rejected if both or neither).
  - `amount` (optional) — defaults to the charge's remaining unrefunded
    balance (full refund). Validated: positive, ≤ remaining.
  - `reverse_transfer` (optional bool) — for destination charges,
    reverses the auto-created Transfer to the connected account.
    Increments `Transfer.amount_reversed`, capped at the transfer's
    remaining unreversed balance. Synthesises a `trr_test_<hex>` ID and
    populates `Refund.source_transfer_reversal`.
  - `refund_application_fee` (optional bool) — flag tracked on the Refund
    object. ApplicationFee is not modelled as a separate resource yet, so
    no additional state changes today.
